Analysis: Love is a well-rounded and explosively athletic running back with a solid build true game-breaking threat every time he touches the ball and the skill set to play every down who figures to make an immediate impact at the next level.

Bailey has an explosive first step a polished pass rush repertoire and a habit of making big plays that will make him an immediate three-down starter with few limitations early on and All-Pro potential in his future.

Downs is a decorated college safety who displays exceptional instincts leadership and physicality while having the versatility to be a difference maker against both the run and pass at the next level.
Reese is a highly explosive and athletic front-7 defender who projects to be a full-time edge at the next level with his explosiveness range and physical play temperament.

With a bruising hand punch stout blocking ability and sheer size and strength Mauigoa figures to be a mauling right tackle with a high ceiling right away at the next level with All-Pro upside if he continues to develop and improve his minor inconsistencies.

Bain is built to be a modern NFL defensive lineman as he can line up anywhere impact the run game on a regular basis collapse the pocket with power and he still has enough burst balance and bend to line up outside and win on clear passing downs.
Styles is a steadily improving off-ball linebacker with exceptional athleticism coverage skills and toughness against the run who still has loads of potential to reach as he moves to the NFL.
Delane is a scheme-versatile corner who has the reactive athleticism ball skills and fluid hip mobility to be a starting corner on Day 1 of his NFL career.
Tyson is an NFL-ready receiver whose clutch ability and contested catch prowess should give him ample opportunity to earn his quarterback’s trust early in his NFL career.

Tate is a smooth and savvy receiver whose strong secure hands make for a QB-friendly target that will see plenty of playing time but his lack of functional strength against press and top-end speed and elusiveness will limit his ceiling at the next level.

Proctor has the ideal size and athleticism combination that teams drool over and despite a few concerns as a pass protector his dominance as a run blocker makes him too tantalizing to last long in the draft.

Woods has a fantastic blend of raw power and explosiveness and his ability to defend the run at a high level creates a high floor on the interior but needs to round out his game and provide more production over potential as a pass rusher.

Terrell has the speed reactive athleticism transition quickness and ball skills wanted in a starting corner at the next level but a lack of play strength and inconsistent effort in the run game could hold him back from reaching a high ceiling.

Fano is a starting-caliber NFL tackle who displays the leverage hand usage and temperament needed for the position but he’ll need to improve his pass pro consistency and add mass to his frame in order to hit his high ceiling.

Brazzell is a height/weight/speed receiver who has the release package explosiveness out of breaks and a large catch radius to make a difference at the next level as long as he takes to an NFL route tree and can improve some after the catch.
Boston is a big-bodied receiver with smooth athleticism who can be a ball-winner in contested catch situations with strong hands and a physical frame.

Cisse is a talented cornerback with speed and athleticism to cover all areas of the field and as he continues to develop physically and instinctually he can be a high-level defender at the next level.
Hood showcases lockdown potential on the outside and if he can round out his game to improve his consistent impact NFL receivers will be on notice.
Ioane is an aggressive guard that has the power and physicality to produce immediately in the run game along with enough foot quickness to mirror interior pass rushers at the next level.

Mesidor is a powerful high-motor EDGE whose suddenness heavy hands and physicality make him a highly-effective pass rusher and if he can improve his effectiveness against the run his potential is through the roof.

Lemon is a sudden receiver with consistent hands that can separate at the LOS in his route and at the catch point making him a prospect that should develop into a passer’s favorite target when they need to make a completion though his lack of catch radius and unpolished skill set could lead to some growing pains early in his career.
Allen is an instinctual quick-reacting off-ball linebacker who can be stout against the run and be a reliable coverage defender at the 2nd level of the defense.

Thieneman is a smart instinctual and versatile leader on the back end of the defense whose excellence in zone coverage and impact at all 3 levels of the field will help him make a difference early on in his career.

McNeil-Warren has the versatility to play both deep safety and hover around the box due to his range instincts physicality and high-level playmaking ability but he will need to improve on his angles and tackling consistency to reach his potential at the next level.

McDonald has what it takes to be a disruptive force at the nose tackle position at the next level through his physical tools play strength and hand usage but he has room to grow as a pass rusher which he should be able to do given his current inexperience.

Hunter is a massive nose tackle with the burst strength and run stuffing ability to be a prototypical nose tackle while he continues to improve his pass rushing impact on his way to a true three-down player.

Mendoza is an experienced quarterback with good height that can make every throw on the field and plays with a consistent confidence that bleeds into his teammates and though he lacks big-time arm strength or athleticism he can lead a team to a championship at the next level.
Sadiq is an athletic competitive smart tight end who has the ability to be a mismatch at the next level but a lack of size and RAC skills could limit his overall effectiveness as an H-only player.

Parker is a powerful and young edge rusher prospect with good length for the position who can contribute on early downs right away while he develops the pass rush repertoire needed to offset his limitations in top-end twitch and bend.
Faulk is an NFL-ready run defender who has the physical tools to be an effective pass rusher if he improves his get-off plays with more purpose and plays with better pad level.

Lomu is a technically-sound pass protector with natural athleticism and polished hand usage and as his body develops and adds more strength to his frame he can be a well-rounded player and a franchise left tackle.

Banks is a strong powerful scheme-versatile defensive lineman with an intriguing combination of strength and explosiveness that allows him to win early and flash dominance but inconsistent pad level and a lack of finishing ability could hold him back early in his career.

Hill will need to develop his abilities in coverage and clean up some over-aggressiveness to reach his ceiling but his football intelligence downhill thumping style and difference-making blitzing ability will make him a starter at the next level.
Branch has the speed and suddenness NFL teams are looking for in a wide receiver and his elite return ability only adds to his value.

Howell has the suddenness hand use and overall pass rush ability to be a difference maker at the next level but his inconsistency against the run makes him a high-end designated pass rusher early in his career.

Height is a slightly undersized explosive athlete who can get after the quarterback however he will have to become more impactful in the run game to reach his full potential as a true every-down starter.

Thomas is a relentless DPR whose get-off quickness explosion suddenness and hand usage make him a threat off the edge as he navigates consistency in the run game due to a lack of length and set-edge ability.

Lane is a long lanky receiver whose strong hands tracking ability and concentration in contested situations make him a vital piece to a receiver room at the next level though a lack of play strength and suddenness could hold him back from reaching his full potential.

Miller is a long trim and athletic tackle who is very technically sound and competitive in both in the run and pass game but he needs to further improve inconsistencies in body control and lower-body flexibility in order to reach his ceiling and be more than a one position player at the next level.

Freeling is an athletic specimen with a very good size a heavy punch and quick feet to warrant a starting spot at the next level. He needs to clean up and tweak some techniques to reach his full potential as a blocker.

Miller lacks the pass rush impact and production to be a three-down player but his violent hand use strong anchor ability and consistently hot motor make him a solid starting-level run defender at the next level.
Jacas is a strong twitchy EDGE who has a developed pass rushing repertoire and should offer immediate versatile value to an NFL team.

Price’s play speed playmaking ability and special teams value will make him an immediate asset to any team but his lack of experience in the passing game will relegate him to being a solid two-down starter at the next level.

McCoy is a versatile corner with good play speed and the upside to be a strong cover corner but his sticky hips and inconsistency locating the football not to mention missing the entire 2025 season could make for a bit more of a learning curve at the next level.
Young is a prototype edge with his size length and strength who can be a high-level run defender and with some refinement to his pass rushing ability can become a difference maker on all 3 downs.

Cooper is a sure-handed receiver with the RAC ability to make a difference as a No. 3 receiver in the NFL but he’ll need to work on his release against press and avoiding contact to take his game to the next level.
Klare is a modern ‘move’ tight end that is at his best when aligned in various spots throughout the game and schemed into advantageous matchups where he can expose a defender’s weakness.

Concepcion is a dynamic athlete who will be a playmaking threat with the ball in his hands as a runner receiver or returner but some inconsistencies catching the ball could hold him back from reaching his potential.

Hecht is a quick technically-sound and fluid-moving center who possesses the skill set to be a starter at the next level but he will need to put on a bit more weight and clean up some of his awareness lapses in pass protection to hit his ceiling.

Dunker is a broad solid guard prospect with the ability to help at tackle in a pinch who excels in the physicality aspects of the game with a fiery demeanor. But he needs to further improve his movement skills in order to maximize his skill set.

Josephs’ length quick get-off and edge setting ability makes him a solid contributor early on in his career but if he can convert some of his length to power refine his PR moves and shore up his eye discipline he can reach a higher ceiling at the next level.

While his lack of size and speed will limit him Rodriguez’s high FBI instincts awareness and on-ball impact should make him a low-end starter on defense and outstanding special teamer at the next level.

Louis is a bit of a tweener who has a safety skill set and linebacker instincts with the all-around athleticism to make an impact in multiple areas at the next level despite lacking true stack-and-shed ability and elite tackling skills.

Lawson is an athletic and instinctive Mike Linebacker with tremendous downhill explosiveness but his size severely hinders his ability to consistently put himself in a position to make tackles and disengage from offensive lineman.
Stowers is an athletic receiving mismatch who will need to improve his strength tenacity and blocking ability to earn the role of a complete tight end in the NFL.

Moore best projects as a designated situational pass rusher due to his athleticism and effective speed-to-power rush but his lack of counter moves and questionable leverage in the run game will limit his ability to be a starting edge rusher.

Bell lacks the consistent effort play speed and overall ability to be a high-end starting-level receiver but his big body and strong hands give him a place in the middle of a receiver rotation at the next level.

Curry is a disciplined high-effort EDGE who displays the pursuit skills and enough pass rush ability to make an impact at the next level though limited functional strength and length could be what holds him back from reaching his potential.
Crawford possesses the first-step explosion and athleticism to be an effective pass rusher at the next level and if he can play with more strength to his frame he can become an every-down starter.
Dennis-Sutton has the length hands and strength to be effective at setting the edge early in his career but he lacks some explosion and athleticism off the edge to consistently rush the passer.

Tiernan is an experienced brute that can move people in the run game and has enough pass protection skills to hold up as a starter on the right side at the next level though his stiffness and high pad level will prevent him from excelling on the outside or sliding into guard.

Bell’s savvy separation and ability to come down with tough catches help him to project as a versatile and valuable receiver but his deficiencies in play strength and overall athletic explosiveness will keep him from becoming a bona fide top option at the next level.

Trotter lacks true sideline-to-sideline range and feel in coverage but his age lineage ability to deconstruct blocks burst and pursuit all give an NFL defensive coordinator traits to develop as a starting-level linebacker.

Trigg has the raw athletic ability catch radius and downhill speed to be a dangerous and versatile weapon immediately but he must refine his blocking fundamentals if wants to carve out a larger role for himself at the next level.

Coleman is a low-center-of-gravity runner who has the contact balance to break tackles in the run game and the natural hands to catch everything thrown his way in the pass game but inconsistent physicality and a lack of breakaway speed could hold him back at the next level.

Though there are non-football/commitment concerns and on-field weaknesses to shore up Hausmann’s intelligence instincts and overall athleticism give him the profile to be a starting linebacker at the next level.

Ponds is an exceptionally tough cornerback prospect with the ability to be both sticky in man coverage and intelligent in zone coverage and has a knack for making plays on the football but will likely be limited to being a slot-only player at the next level due to his deficiencies in overall size and length.
Rivers is a naturally reactive athlete with quick COD ability and remarkable coverage instincts and while he lacks elite size he is ultra competitive and can affect the game in a variety of ways.

Simpson is a sufficient athlete with toughness and leadership but is relatively inexperienced as a starter and can struggle with his accuracy which will require time and the right situation to fully develop into his potential as a quality starting QB.
Bernard is a savvy route runner with strong hands and sturdy frame that allows him to play with physicality after the catch but lacks that top gear to threaten the defense vertically.
Abney is a sticky and physical outside corner with impressive ball skills who will cause trouble for NFL wide receivers if he improves his technique.
Pregnon is an experienced and massive interior presence in the trenches who has the power and strength in the run and pass game to be a quality starter early in his NFL career.
Everette is an athletic press-friendly outside cornerback who offers a tempting skill set and could become a complete cornerback with a few adjustments.
Ramsey is a fluid versatile safety who assuming he can clean up tackling and aggression concerns in the run game has the potential to be a high-end cover safety at the next level.

Bisontis plays with great pad level and a firm anchor as a pass protector while also being light on his feet showing the ability to fit into any run scheme making him a plug and play blocker in the interior at the next level.
Kilgore is a long and versatile defensive back with good range and ball skills in coverage and if he plays with more physicality he can be a consistent contributor for a defense at the next level.
Scott is a fiery smart rangy and physical slot corner who should make a difference at the next level but he’ll need to improve his eye discipline and tackle efficiency to reach his full potential.

Jones will be a reliable and consistent presence in the middle of any offensive line due to his athleticism footwork and effort but he does need to get stronger in his lower half and core if he wants to reach his true ceiling.

Johnson is a smart athletic and technically-sound corner who can be sticky in zone coverage and shows patience in press to be a contributor on the outside but he will need to work on his play strength and abilities in off-man to round out his skill set.
Igbinosun is a long athletic corner who plays with physicality but has been plagued by mental errors that need to be ironed out of his game for him to be able to reach his full potential.

Speed is the name of Thompson’s game and he pairs that with high-end short-area quickness and tracking skills but his small stature and limited route tree could be something that holds him back at the next level.

Lew is a smart experienced center with very good awareness that has the upper-body strength to be a reliable short-set anchor but his lack of tenacity vertical push and ultimately positional flexibility will limit his ceiling at the next level.

Fields is a prototypical X-receiver who is an immediate red-zone threat due to his size and contested catch ability but his lack of high-level twitch and long speed limit his ability to be a consistent separator down the field.
Sarratt is a long physical receiver who lacks the deep speed to be a consistent starter but if he can develop his route tree and sure up his route running he could reach a higher ceiling.

Neal has the comfort in press and the ball skills and tackling aggression to carve out a role on the boundary at the next level but his lack of top speed and slow transition will limit his overall usage.
Smith is a true centerfield prospect who was clutch in his do-it-all role in college and if he can improve his tackling and eye discipline he should see meaningful reps at the next level.
Rutledge is a tough physical and nasty finisher in the run game but he’ll need to improve his hand consistency and leverage in pass pro to become more than just a low-end single-position starter.

Clark is the definition of a ballhawk and has the hands and closing speed to make impact plays as a turnover machine but his thin frame and lack of impact in the run game limit his appeal as a starting safety early in his career.

Wheatley possesses the speed range and ball skills to be a safety blanket for any defense but he will need to improve his tackling and play with more physicality to blossom into a versatile chess piece for any defensive coordinator.

Lance is a big fast receiver who can become a deep threat at the next level with his tracking ability but his questionable hands linear frame and lack of creation ability after the catch suggest he’s got work to do to hit his high ceiling.
Brailsford is undersized and lacks position flexion but his hand usage intelligence technique and motor will help him to function as a starting center at the next level.

Huskey is a versatile ball-hawking free safety with very good range that can make an impact as a deep defender and an open-field tackler right away and has the potential to be a true 3-level impact safety.

Coleman is a twitchy sudden receiver with dependable hands and strong RAC/return ability to make it as a role-playing starter at the next level but his frail frame combined with limited play strength and struggles vs. physical corners could hold him back.
Moore is an experienced consistent and opportunistic safety who shows quality instincts in coverage while also being a quality run defender on the back end.

Lee is a long corner who excels in press man coverage loves to be physical with receivers and has plus ball skills at the catch point but lack of position flexion and an inconsistent motor against the run will limit him to being a No.3 outside-only corner at the next level.

While Slaughter’s clunky lower body lack of natural popping power and inability to stay low due to his length will cause hesitation on his athletic profile some teams will form a line for his fast processing experience and impenetrable pass pro mindset.

Schrauth is an intelligent and technical guard who plays with toughness and nuance to go with his solid frame but limits in power flexibility and reactive athleticism will limit him to being a one-position player at the next level.
Reed-Adams is a large experienced guard with a mean streak who will need to play with better hand discipline and foot quickness to earn a starting role at the next level.
Campbell has the size and strength to be solid in pass protection and a people mover in the run game but his wide hand placement limited flexibility and penalty issues could hold him back.

Haulcy is an instinctive safety with a compact frame demonstrating natural ball skills and a physical play style but he will need to be more consistent as a tackler in space to become a true every-down player.

Iheanachor needs to improve his technique consistency particularly with drop-back speed hand placement and timing but his combination of size athleticism and strength make him an intriguing project for a team willing to invest the time.

Nussmeier has the leadership qualities and deep passing ability to make it as a quality backup at the next level but he’ll need to improve his decision making and footwork if he wants to become a starting-level player.

Klubnik has the arm talent athleticism and composure in the pocket to be an eventual starter at the next level but he will need to clean up his occasional lapses in mechanics strengthen his progressions and reading of defensive rotations to reach his full potential.
Big Citrus' is an early-down run plugger who displays raw power and explosion in his hands to cause disruption along the interior

Barber is a big-framed tackle with good length and functional strength in his body who excels in the run game but needs to keep developing his hand placement and body control as he transitions to the next level.

Crownover is a rare case of natural and controlled athleticism trapped in a massive frame who is still developing his technical skills as a pass protector but has the tools to be a quality swing tackle with the right development program behind him.

Shelton is an athletic mover who possesses the range athleticism and intelligence to be a solid backup OT but he needs to improve his overall play strength if he’s going to compete with elite NFL talent on a consistent basis.
World has the physical tools to warrant serious NFL looks but he must improve his hand use foot quickness and pad level to be seen as a reliable NFL starter.
Golday possesses the range speed and size many teams are looking for in their linebackers and he should provide value while his instincts develop.

Parker is a smart strong and disciplined offensive lineman who will fit best as a versatile interior offensive lineman given how his lack of length and also his heavy feet will make it more difficult for him to hold up on the edge.
Lawrence is a twitchy pass rushing prospect with a developed skill set who possesses a high ceiling if he can improve his stoutness at the point of attack and become a more reliable tackler.

Zuhn is an incredibly experienced lineman with versatility who displays great pass protection and anchor ability but his lack of arm length and foot speed will best suit him as a quality backup along the interior at the next level with spot-starting potential.
Stukes is a lengthy corner with strong closing speed and ball skills but his instincts and mirroring ability have room for improvement as he looks to become a consistent starter at the next level.

Beck is an experienced quarterback who can play in structure with good pocket awareness and sufficient processing ability but his struggles under pressure and limited arm talent create a low ceiling for him as a passer at the next level.

Singleton has the size speed and third-down ability to be a solid starter and kick returner at the next level but it remains to be seen if he can be effective on poorly-blocked plays gain extra yards against bigger opponents and handle a backfield by himself.
Washington is a big back with an explosive blend of size and speed to reach the open field but he lacks some elusiveness and playmaking ability to be a consistent early-down starter in the run game.

Allar has high-end arm strength size and ability to make difficult throws look easy but needs to be a more polished passer in the pocket with improved footwork and touch to become a starting-level QB.
Stephens is a powerful mauling left guard who looks to finish his blocks but has a limited ceiling due to some physical limitations and struggles blocking more athletic defenders in open space.

Overton is an experienced and versatile defensive lineman who will likely see more snaps on the interior at the next level with good play strength and violence to defend the run but he lacks the explosion and pass rush juice to be a consistent three-down player.
Gulbin is a smart interior blocker who displays good hand usage and his many sufficient traits for the position make him a reliable backup with potential starter upside at the next level.

Burton is a smart technically-sound interior offensive lineman with true position flexibility who currently lacks the power COD skills and the ability to finish blocks with intent to be an NFL starter.

Johnson has the speed vision and catching ability to help an NFL offense but he will need to improve his strength in his lower body and technique in pass protection if he wants to reach his ceiling of being an every-down starter at the next level.

Bowry is a tackle with positional versatility who has the athleticism quickness and anchor to make it at the next level but late hands limited flexibility and questionable reactive ability keep him from being a starting-level player early on.
Payne offers value in a backup role given his athletic profile willingness to play in any role and ability to disrupt the play though he will need to develop his play recognition and discipline.

Davis is an experienced and dependable prospect that wins with technique who has physical limitations but with proper development and some technical refinement could carve out a nice role on an NFL roster.

Barham is a bit of a tweener who is transitioning to be an edge rusher full time with high-end speed and quickness off the edge but will need to become a stronger run defender to be a true three-down player.
Canady has the quick twitch movements and athleticism to be a versatile coverage defender at the next level but needs to play with more physicality to be a true three-down nickel.

Barton offers value as a versatile defender but his lack of a pass rush plan ineffective pass rush moves and hesitation to make plays in the run game may limit him to a backup role on passing downs early in his career.

Willis is a strong run blocker with active hands and the ability to consistently drive defenders off the ball but his inconsistent anchor and functional strength need to improve to become a starting-level player.
Allen can create separation using his short-area quickness route feel or long speed which will allow him to see the field early while he earns more reps.

Cameron’s route running and special teams ability should allow him to compete for NFL snaps but his lackluster long speed and inability to consistently win one-on-one situations will likely limit his chances of being a consistent starter at the next level.
Jackson is a nose tackle prospect with impressive physical traits and heavy hands that will need to become more consistent with his pad level to become the starting-caliber player he is capable of.

Williams is a shifty receiver who fits best in a slot and gadget-type role in the NFL but deficiencies in straight-line speed size and lack of experience with an NFL route tree will limit him at the next level.

Durant is an undersized defensive tackle who wins with an explosive first step and initial punch accompanied by good agility and balance but his shortcomings in stoutness against double teams block shedding and pass rush effectiveness will limit him to being a backup one-gap penetrator at the next level.

Jackson has the physical tools to be a lockdown cornerback and he flashes the potential to live up to that role but he needs to improve his reactive footwork eye discipline and learn to swallow up space in coverage to show more boom than bust.
Hudson is a savvy receiver with enough size and speed to be a solid rotational player and special teamer at the next level if he can improve his tracking high-pointing and explosiveness.

Hall has the awareness and coverage skills to be a solid backup outside corner at the next level in a zone-heavy defense while showing the required willingness to defend the run and be a special teams contributor.
Moore is a long corner with good instincts and awareness but a lanky build lack of play strength and injury concerns may limit his overall ceiling at the next level.

Brinson wins with a varied release package route tempo and speed making him a deep threat with starting-level potential but he’ll need to improve his body control toughness and effort to reach that ceiling.

Stribling has the size speed tracking skills and strong hands to be a solid depth receiver at the next level but his lack of true separation skills especially against physical defenders could hold him back early in his career.

Robertson looks the part of an NFL passer and makes throws that suggest a top draft pick but his inconsistencies and penchant for giving the ball away late in games will prevent him from ever making a consistent impact as the leader of an offense.

Virgil is a long receiver with a thin frame and natural hands who wins with his savvy and skilled approach but his shortcomings in suddenness and play strength could hinder him from reaching his ceiling at the next level.

Keenan is a massive presence in the middle of the defensive line that can plug up run lanes and eat double teams but his lack of production as a pass rusher will limit him to a backup role at the next level.

Marshall is a physical outside corner that has the ability to disrupt passes at the catch point and can match up with a variety of receivers but his lack of size high-end speed and technique issues make him a better backup option than a starting-level player.
Burks is a quick and twitchy receiver who projects to be a backup but has the playmaking ability to be a game-breaker and create explosive plays at the next level.

Daniels is still learning the true linebacker position making his stack-and-shed ability and instincts in the box suspect but his versatility stemming from his safety background make him an intriguing option to develop at the next level.
Caldwell lacks the polish some prospects offer but his upside is clear given his frame and athleticism giving teams comfort as they wait for him to develop.
Gyllenborg has the size athleticism and physicality to carve out playing time at the next level but he will need to clean up his blocking techniques and leverage to raise his overall game.

Law is a gadget player who has the speed burst acceleration and RAC ability to do damage with the ball in his hands but limited separation skills and a minimalistic route tree will present growing pains at the next level.

Demmings is a wiry twitched-up corner with good height and length that can be effective immediately at the next level on the outside in a press-man defense though his lack of play recognition and inability to change directions limit his versatility and reliability.

Williams is a competitive rangy EDGE whose non-stop motor and active hands make him an intriguing rotational player at the next level as a lack of consistent pass rush ability and setting the edge limit his ability to become a starter early on.

Rivers has a clear trait working in his favor in his speed and he has shown rapid development over 3 seasons as a full-time receiver but he will need to stay on this trajectory to overcome his lack of size strength and YAC creation.
Muhammad is a corner with good size and some athletic ability though his inability to mirror receivers and his penchant for grabbing will likely limit him to an outside corner in a zone-based scheme.
Bentley is a big-bodied versatile tight end with soft hands and functional athleticism whose receiving value is ahead of his ability to impose his will as an in-line blocker.

Claiborne’s burst acceleration and elusiveness are traits to build off of but his lack of control as a runner limited pass game impact and ball security concerns make him a backup role runner at the next level.

Harris is a massive versatile defensive lineman who displays a long first step and the ability to work laterally but a high pad level being on the ground too much and limited agility make it an uphill battle for him to succeed at the next level.

Anthony’s prototypical size frame and length along with his quickness off the ball will get him looks as a solid rotational defender at the next level but his stiffness and lack of functional strength currently limit his upside.
Reiger is an explosive athlete who can be a quality depth piece to a pass rush unit off the edge but must improve his play strength to be a consistent three-down player.

Benny has the quick hands and the play strength to carve out playing time at the next level but his lack of explosiveness off the line and inconsistencies shedding blocks will limit his overall usage.
Fisher has the experience natural instinct and communication skills of a veteran Mike linebacker but his frame limitations will make it difficult to be an every-down player.

Halton is a traditional penetrating 3-technique that can win at the snap with quickness and hand use overwhelming his opponent on the way to the passer but his lack of bulk play recognition and a secondary pass rush will prevent him from becoming more than a rotational depth piece.

Hurst is a long-framed prospect who has a purposeful release and a knack for finding the holes in the defense but he will need to maximize his catch opportunities and put in more effort in the run game to reach his full potential.

Onyedim is an athletic defensive tackle who displays flashes of high-end twitch and athleticism along the interior as a pass rusher but needs to get stronger as a run defender to reach his full potential.
Pounds is a left tackle who shows good strength and leg drive in the run game but his slower footwork and mediocre hip flexibility in pass protection will keep him as a backup.

Payton lacks the fluid movement ability and quick wins to warrant a starting role early in his career but his ability to disrupt throwing lanes and effort should afford him a backup rotational position on an NFL roster.
Young is a big receiver with good route running ability strong hands and a wide catch radius but lacks the consistent separation skills and varied release package to make him more than a top backup.
Casey is a tackle who possesses good athleticism and intelligence but will need to overcome some physical limitations and refine techniques to become a more complete blocker at the next level.

Masses is an intriguing cornerback prospect with reactive athleticism press-man coverage ability and ball skills though his lanky build limited strength and tendency to grab in coverage likely hold him back from being a starter early in his career.

Boettcher is a high-motor naturally instinctive Mike Linebacker who is comfortable in coverage and has the tools to be a quality special teams player but needs to improve his take-on ability to be a true three-down player at the next level.
Allen’s aggressive running style and workhorse ability should allow him to be a quality backup in the NFL but his limited pass game impact and playmaking ability will limit his role.

Tucker is a pass rush specialist with a plus motor who consistently gets after the passer and makes his presence felt but his small stature and struggles in the run game will limit his ability to be a starter at the next level.
Corleone will need to regain his quickness and develop a more NFL-ready pass rushing attack to see a high volume of impactful snaps at the next level.

Proctor is an explosive and agile 3-tech who gets into the backfield against both the pass and the run but his lack of stoutness will keep him off the field on early downs and absence of a counter move limits his immediate pass rush impact.

Hutchins is an athletic EDGE who has active hands a hot motor and good first-step explosion but his limited pass rush repertoire and lack of functional strength to set the edge with consistency make him a top backup at the next level.
Prysock is a long corner with solid athleticism who excels in press-man coverage but his elongated transition ability and struggles at the catch point will keep him as a backup at the next level.
Elarms-Orr is an athletic rangy prospect who needs to play with far better eye discipline and play recognition to earn more reps at the next level.
Lucas is a physical specimen with tremendous length and powerful upper-body strength to be disruptive off the edge but lacks the consistency and production to be a full-time starter.
Johnson is an instinctive physical ball hawk with proven production but his size and high-end speed limitations likely confine him to a backup role.
Davis is a super long corner who has the size burst and tackling skills to develop into a solid NFL player but his lanky build and sticky hips could hold him back from being a starting-level player.

Raridon is a complete tight end that lacks some receiving upside due to a lack of suddenness but with the addition of good weight to complement his frame and upside as a blocker he can carve out a consistent role at the next level.

Fuller is a big physical cornerback who uses his length to stay attached to receivers and can get his nose dirty in the run game but his lack of high-end ball skills and shortcomings as a reactive athlete will keep him from starting early in his career.

Barrett is a disciplined and strong nose tackle who wins with hand use and intelligence but a high pad level lack of a pass rush repertoire and first-step length will force him to a backup role at the NFL level.

Range and instincts help mask the few flaws in York's game such as his frame and stoutness against the run game but there is no doubt that a defense will be properly lined up because of the communication and leadership he displays on the field.

Kelly has the tools to be an intriguing passing-down linebacker due to his ability to rush the passer and natural zone eyes but his tight hips lack of run feel and tackling issues will prevent him from seeing every-down playing time.

Burke has intriguing size and length that blends with lateral mobility and a hot motor but his limited pass rush ability and inconsistent pad level likely make him more of a rotational defender early on in his career.

Bissainthe is an athletic rangy and smart middle linebacker whose lack of stack-and-shed ability and occasional lapses in eye discipline as a cover defender make him a backup early on and ultimately cap his ceiling at the next level.

Green is a raw prospect despite his collegiate experience who displays high-end arm strength mobility and size that is hard to find for the position. However his decision making and overall accuracy needs to be improved upon to be more than a talented backup with untapped potential.

Brown has the top-end speed to take the top off and stretch defenses vertically but his struggles in creating separation through press and route savviness will limit him to a gadget player at the next level.

Delp is a sure-handed tight end who has positional versatility and mismatch potential but his lack of technique and strength as a blocker will hold him back from seeing starting snaps at the next level early in his career.

Nwankpa projects as an “old school” backup strong safety who will bring juice to special teams at the next level but his limited speed and hip fluidity will prevent him from being a true three-down player.
Whittington is an experienced player with natural running back instincts like good vision foot quickness and ball security but lacks the size and power to be a starting-level running back.

Black isn’t the most physically imposing back but his competitiveness footwork and contact balance as a runner are quality traits that can be valuable as a depth piece in a running back room at the next level.
Wisniewski is a tough smart downhill safety who can carve out an early-down role at the next level but his lack of overall speed and range will limit him to mostly special teams usage.
McClellan has the size strength and discipline to anchor an NFL interior front but inconsistent pad level and first-step quickness may cap his overall potential.

Slone plays out of control and misses too many tackle attempts but his relentless attitude physicality and discipline make him an intriguing development option as a backup EDGE and special teamer early on.

Jones has the blend of size strength and athleticism to develop at the next level but his struggles with consistent blocking leverage and technical skills against speed will limit him to a backup role.

Capehart has shown flashes of brilliance along the defensive front due to his play strength and heavy hands but an inconsistent pad level and limited impact as a pass rusher make him a backup defensive tackle early on at the next level.

Brevard has starting level upside as a true two-down run stuffer with upside as a pass rusher due to heavy hands and power at the POA but body control issues an inconsistent pad level and limited range make him a solid backup NT.

Koziol is a massive target who has the hands 50/50-win skills and mismatch potential to eventually become a starting-level player but his massive struggles as a blocker currently limit him to a backup role.

Benson is a deep threat that has enough quickness to win at the LOS and the second gear to separate vertically though his inability to separate elsewhere and a general lack of receiver skills will prevent him from becoming more than a depth piece at the next level.

Dixon is a versatile competitive cornerback with solid speed and closing burst but a lack of play strength tackle efficiency and consistent willingness in run support limit him to be a top backup at the next level.

With his size athleticism reliable hands and competitive blocking skills Hibner should become a complete tight end at the next level but limited block sustain and inconsistent separation skills keep him from being a starting-level player.

Phillips is a fundamentally-sound corner with smooth footwork patience and ball skills to carve out a role at the next level but his lack of top-end speed and struggles with having disciplined vision will limit his ceiling.

Henry has a compact frame low center of gravity and good vision that provide value as a runner but he offers little value on 3rd down given he is not a natural hands-catcher and lacks stoutness in pass protection.

Daniels is a reliable WR with strong hands an understanding of how to get open and a willingness to block but he lacks a second gear to create much separation or yards after the catch limiting him to a depth-level player.

McGowan is a physically-gifted back with the skill set that revolves around his explosive first-step athleticism and contact balance but his control as a runner and limited potential in the passing game make him a backup at the next level.

Moore is a versatile EDGE whose effort and play strength give him a chance at the next level but he’ll need to improve his consistency as a pass rusher and feel off the ball in order to be more than a depth-level player.
Owens is an aggressive downhill runner that has the patience burst and footwork to carve out a role but doesn’t offer much in the open field or as a tool in the pass game to be more than a backup.

Moss is a tough physical downhill back that can carve out an early-down role as a backup at the next level but with inconsistency as a blocker in pass pro and lack of top-end speed will limit his overall usage.
Royer is a solid and athletically-built tight end who is a reliable pass catcher and talented movement blocker in space to be a quality backup at the next level.

Taylor is a shorter thickly-built running back who possesses the contact balance and pass game upside to make it as a backup at the next level but an upright running style and a lack of long speed or power will limit his ceiling.

Faison possesses the vision and contact balance to be a solid backup two-down runner but a lack of breakaway speed elite elusiveness in the open field and high-end pass game impact will limit his usage at the next level.
Hemby is a tough downhill one-cut runner who shows sufficient vision but lacks the explosiveness and elusiveness to be a consistent playmaker at the next level.
Even with some athletic and explosive limitations Boerkircher has the FBI hands and competitive toughness to be a quality No. 2 backup at the next level.

Miller is a compact muscular back who has the vision contact balance and ball security to be trusted as an early-down runner but does not have the burst or juice in the passing game to project as more than a backup two-down runner at the next level.

While Perkins possesses the athletic gifts and timely playmaking ability coveted by NFL teams his passive approach poor eye discipline and error-prone tendencies in 2025 suggest a backup role until these concerns are addressed.

Kacmarek has the tools to be one of the better run blocking tight ends in the league and while he isn’t a high-end pass catching threat his skill set can complement a tight end room at the next level very well.

Gentry’s lack of stoutness and inability to efficiently shed in the run game will limit his usage at the next level but his massive size length and range make him an intriguing prospect for personnel rotation.

Gill-Howard’s effort is undeniable and his active hands allow him to chain together pass rush moves easily but the lack of gap discipline and elite play strength/anchor will make it hard for him to see the field early on in his career.

Thomas is a big rangy safety who has the upside to match up well in coverage but major inconsistencies as a tackler and late read-and-react ability make him more of a depth-level defender and strong special teamer early on in his career.
Rolder is a steady and reliable second-level defender who shows the instincts range and competitive mentality to be a quality backup and core-four special teams contributor.
Fitzgerald has the ability in coverage and turnover production to be a backup free safety at the next level but lacks the physicality to become a three-down starter.

Joly is a prototypical H-TE that offers receiving upside and alignment versatility but his lack of a true TE route tree and inconsistent blocking technique make him more of a No. 2 option than a starter early in his career.

Fagnano is an experienced quarterback prospect with plus decision making ability and clutch tendencies but his limited ceiling as a passer and a runner will likely keep him to a backup role at the next level.

Payton is a gritty dual-threat quarterback whose ability to extend plays and generate yards on the ground often mask his rigid throwing mechanics and his struggles to read defenses and make the right decision.

Stroman is a physical albeit smaller strong safety who has the desired combination of speed intensity and tackling ability to find success at the next level but his upside could be limited due to a lack of production at the 3rd level.

Cuevas is a versatile tight end with smooth athleticism and can be a reliable target in the short-to-intermediate passing game but he lacks some play strength and power as a blocker to be an every-down contributor.

Wright is a versatile corner with good route recognition skills and run game ability who will need to improve at locating the ball and being less handsy down the field in order to become more than a depth-level defender and special teamer.
Wagner is a towering tackle with good length and power in his upper body but his pad level and flexibility need to be improved upon as his body keeps developing at the next level.

Roush is a physical tight end who looks to finish blocks to the the ground and thrives in short-yardage situations where he can create separation on short routes or create large run gaps but ultimately his inconsistency catching the football and lack of separation on medium and deep routes will keep him from becoming a starter.

Klein projects as an in-line tight end with good blocking upside while having the speed and athleticism to develop into a mismatch in the passing game but needs to round out his skill set to be an every-down contributor.
Spears-Jennings is a physical hard-hitting safety who’s best coming downhill in run support although his delayed instincts and over-aggression in the run game will keep him as a back up.
Robinson is an explosive and versatile defensive tackle that wins with quickness and leverage but his limited size and stoutness will ultimately keep him in a reserve role early in his career.

One of the largest human beings in  football Bell has the length strength and power to develop into a starting-level player but lacking foot quickness and late hands will make him a work in progress early on.

Kanak shows the speed route savvy and competitive toughness as a blocker to make it at the next level and with more strength and experience at the position he has a chance to ascend to a starting-level player.

Llewellyn rushes the passer with a high motor technical hand use and a respectable repertoire to be a backup defensive end in an even-front scheme but must improve his play strength and edge set to be an every-down player.
Cleveland is a big agile nose tackle who can win with strength at the next level but his lack of explosiveness and pass rush ability make him better suited as a backup early on.

Murdock has the stoutness shed ability and nose for the ball to be a physical two-down Mike LB at the next level but his limited movement ability and deficiencies in the pass game will put a cap on his ceiling.
Elliott may lack the passing game impact and quick block shedding to warrant a starting spot but his toughness and reliability will make him a dependable option to fill out the roster.

Bryan Thomas Jr. is a twitchy high-motor edge rusher whose speed and get-off make him a depth-level option for a defensive line but a lack of length pass rush moves and strength to set the edge will make it hard on him at the next level.

Despite being slightly undersized Nowakowski is a tremendous blocker who has good play strength natural hands and competitive toughness but has a limited route tree and struggles to consistently create separation.

Lacy is an elusive and versatile weapon who has the top-end speed to be a gadget player and returner at the next level but lacks the elite physical tools to effectively contribute in contested situations or as a blocker in the run game to be more than a depth-level receiver.

McAlister utilizes his size at the catch point and is a YAC monster due to his contact balance but his inconsistent hands and mediocre separation ability will keep him from seeing the field as a starter.

Nwokobia’s communication skills zone play and physicality are all good enough for him to earn playing time but his lack of top-end speed and man coverage skills limit his ability to be an every-down contributor.
Jones is a big long EDGE who has the get-off and burst to cause disruption but is limited by his tight hips lack of PR repertoire and inability to set the edge with consistency against the run.

Endries has smooth hands for a tight end to go with the play speed and competitiveness desired at the position but his narrow build and limited play strength cap his upside as an in-line blocker. That combined with his shortcomings as a pure separator will limit him to being a backup H-tight end at the next level.
Sapp has an NFL-ready frame and will offer value as a backup run defender and special teamer while he continues to work on his pass rushing ability.

Taaffe is a slightly undersized safety with some athletic limitations that cap his ceiling but his toughness smarts and leadership qualities should earn him a long NFL career as a special teams ace and locker room guy.

McMurray is a slot corner whose solid tackling closing speed and toughness make him a core special teamer and depth-level defender as his lack of ball skills and transition quickness limit his appeal as a starter.

Vandeross is an experienced and effective slot receiver with the twitch route savvy and well-rounded skill set to find a role at the next level but his small frame and lack of long speed will make it tough to see the field consistently.

Tomczak has the technical route running ability and strong reliable hands to carve out a role at the next level but his lack of playmaking ability with the ball and consistent separation against press will limit his overall usage.
Traore is an athletic receiving option who has natural hands but his liabilities as a blocker and limitations in a special teams role limit his overall appeal.
Regis has the skill set to be a solid run-stuffing nose tackle but his lack of production and inability to be a force on 3rd down will keep him as a backup at the next level.

With his ability after the catch and consistent hands to move the chains Wright can be a trusted target for any quarterback but he needs to play with more physicality to his frame to become more than a backup at the next level.

Austin is a speedy corner who can run with just about anyone with good transition ability and sufficient ball skills but his lean frame and tendency to grab may limit his ability to be an every-down player.

Jamison-Travis is a prototypical two-gapping nose tackle who utilizes his arm length and size at the line of scrimmage to stack and find the ball but his lack of pass rush ability and stamina make him a two-down backup.
Kamara has a ton of experience and production as a pass rusher but his lack of size play strength and athleticism will limit his potential at the next level.

Hayes is an explosive athlete that also blends good power and hand use in his pass rushing ability but his inconsistencies in edge setting against the run and limited bend will limit him to a backup role in the NFL.
Eastern is a big-bodied space eater with good play strength and anchor ability who with some technical refinements could be a solution to a leaky run defense.
Smith is a space-eating nose tackle who can plug gaps in the run game but lacks the athleticism and pass rush ability to be a three-down contributor at the next level.
Douglas has the size and body control to be a red-zone threat but lacks the strength and twitchiness to consistently separate at an NFL-starter level.
Fano gives good effort and has enough length to intrigue NFL teams but his overall lack of winning plays in either the passing or running game limits his initial appeal as he continues to develop.

Styles is a versatile defensive back who will play a significant role on special teams with his speed and athleticism but needs to be more physical and instinctive to carve out a bigger role at the next level.

Thomas is a big-bodied but wiry receiver who possesses a large catch radius and contested-catch ability but a lack of play strength COD skills and route diversity likely hold him back from being a starting-level player at the next level.
Henderson is a vertical threat with explosive top-end speed whose upside hinges on developing reliable hands and refining route running.

Bond is versatile gives maximum effort on the field and displays reliable hands but his release inconsistency trouble making catches in traffic and mediocre separation ability will make him a better backup than starter early in his career.
Rowser is an intriguing defensive back who will have to show that his versatility physicality and upside outweigh the concerns over his eye discipline and technique.
Roberts lacks the ability to create easy separation and is not a natural playmaker which will be tough for the older draft prospect to overcome despite his route savvy and tempo.

Sorey is an athletic defender with a unique skill set to be a third-down sub-package linebacker with his range and blitz ability but lacks the true take-on ability and tackling consistency to be an every-down player.
Fordham is a MLB who shows solid range pursuit and stoutness to be a depth-level option at the next level but his lack of tackle efficiency length and feel in coverage could ultimately hold him back.
Metayer has the size and receiving ability to be a solid backup TE and special teams player but his elusiveness and inconsistent blocking may prevent him from being a consistent starter.

Herron possesses the length size and power desired for the position but lacks some technical refinement and has inconsistencies with pad level and hand usage which needs development to become a quality backup at the next level.

Marion is an explosive receiver whose athleticism RAC ability and competitiveness should allow him to make an NFL roster but a lack of consistent separation skills and limited production in contested situations make him a depth-level option.
Thomas is a versatile gadget receiver with breakaway speed and playmaking ability on special teams but his inconsistent hands and route savvy must improve to see a larger role in an offense.
Wetjen possesses the speed explosiveness and playmaking ability to play a specific and limited role in offense but will make a big impact at the next level as a return specialist.

McRee is a backup Y-tight end at the next level with his reliability and awareness as a run blocker but his lack of quickness elite play strength and overall athleticism will limit his usage at the next level.
Fa’amoe has the power and leg drive to be a mover in the run game but his lack of lateral redirection and inconsistent latch will limit him to a backup role at the next level.
Singleton is a big safety with good length and instincts on the back end but doesn’t play as physical as his frame would suggest and lacks some COD quickness to recover.

Bell brings a well-rounded skill set as a wide receiver with the toughness and attitude that will allow him to help on special teams and in dirty work situations but lacks the twitchiness and high-end traits to see consistent time as a starter at the next level.
Anderson is a tough compact slot-only receiver with good route savvy but his limited separation ability and inconsistent hands will limit his role at the next level.
Altmyer is a smart and tough-minded QB who shows good qualities in being a backup QB at the next level despite his lack of arm talent and true playmaking ability.

McCutchin is a lengthy athlete who has the physical tools to play on the outside in the modern game but his limited instincts ball skills and impatience will require patience from whichever team employs him.
Kelly is a long edge rusher who plays outside his range but his lack of fluidity and issues finishing plays will limit him to a backup role in the NFL.

While his natural abilities such length play strength and long speed will hinder his potential Smith is a smooth off-ball defender who excels in zone coverage and can function as a backup both on the inside and outside.

Guilbeau is a smaller corner with clear limitations in his game but he has the requisite comfort in zone coverage and the willingness and ability in run support to earn a coach’s trust and a role on a team.
Burke has the desirable frame and underlying athleticism teams bank on while he works to improve his anchor stoutness pad level and mirroring fluidity.

Pride has the speed burst and zone coverage skills to make the roster as a depth-level corner but his sticky hips and struggles in man or press will make it difficult for him to ascend to being a starter.

Woodaz’s length and range in pass coverage and versatility on 3rd downs will get him in the door at the next level but his inability to quickly diagnose what’s going on in the run game or stack-and-shed bigger blockers will make it difficult to see the field.
Williams has the athletic profile to be a coverage linebacker in a backup capacity but needs to improve as a tackler and take-on ability to earn more playing time at the next level.

M’ba is a big strong and disciplined defensive lineman who brings scheme versatility but an upright pad level a lack of agility and inconsistency in the pass game make him a depth-level option early in his career.
Harvey possesses the zone coverage skills burst and ball skills to be given a shot as a depth corner but he lacks the hip transition and overall speed to ascend to starting-level status.

Jefferson is a high-motor athletic linebacker with sufficient coverage skills who can be a quality core-four special teamer but lacks the size and frame to be a consistent run defender at the next level.

Gusta is a strong interior defender whose first-step quickness and motor shine as a depth-level backup but his lack of leverage as a rusher and inconsistency finishing plays limit his effectiveness at the next level.

Obiazor offers value given his block shedding inside the box and zone awareness but his slow play recognition and struggles matching up with NFL-caliber athletes will keep him in a backup role to begin his NFL career.
Scott is a compactly-built free safety showing leadership and ball skills on the back-end of the defense who has the traits to be a quality special teams and depth piece in the secondary.

West lacks ideal height bend and play strength for the next level but his quick first step powerful hands and hot motor make him a solid rotational defensive line option and occasional special teamer.

Hall has the get-off quickness and first-step length that are sufficient for the next level but an upright pad level limited pass rush ability and overall inconsistency make him more of a deep rotational option.
Thompson’s size length and tackling ability allow him to serve a role as a backup run defender but his pass rushing ability will prevent him from being a truly effective option for teams.

Smith is an athletic cornerback with the length and skill set to play both on the inside and outside but his limited ball skills hip fluidity and value as a run defender will prevent him from being a consistent contributor at the next level.

Lafayette shows potential as a versatile defender who plays with aggression and a sound understanding of his role but his limited hip fluidity ball skills and head-on tackling concerns will leave him fighting for a roster spot.
Walthall offers the speed route urgency and body control to be a deep threat but his inconsistent hands and lack of special teams appeal will move him to a backup role at the next level.

King is an older injury-susceptible quarterback without an elite arm proper decision making or next-level accuracy but is a team leader whose rushing and improvisational skills could be enticing enough to warrant further development.
McCulley is a big-framed receiver with natural athleticism and the ability to win at the catch point but lacks the separation and route running precision to be a true every-down type of receiver.

Moses is a small but scrappy safety whose production and physicality at all levels will give him a shot at the next level but a lack of feel as a deep defender and incredibly inconsistent tackling form/production make it an uphill battle.
Wallace shows the body control and tracking ability to be a depth WR but must improve his play strength and release quickness at the next level.
Sturdivant has the size speed tracking and hands to develop at the next level but his lack of playmaking ability and consistent separation will keep him down on the depth chart early in his career.

Webb possesses the ideal first-step length and play strength to become a versatile depth-level defender but his inefficiency against double teams and inconsistency rushing the passer could hold him back.

Anthony is an average receiver who is sufficient in most areas of the game with solid hands and run-after-catch ability but his lack of route savvy and contested-catch ability will limit his role at the next level.

Marshall's campaign for a rotational defensive tackle spot hinges on proving his agility and heavy hands can offset his limited impact plays underdeveloped pass-rush plan and lack of lateral stoutness.

Graves is a solidly-built interior defender who has a quick first step and explosive hands to defeat blocks quickly against the run and pass but needs to improve his functional strength and run anchor as he moves to the next level.
Key is a big wide receiver who shows strong hands savviness as a route runner and competitiveness as a blocker to be a depth piece in the wide receiver room at the next level.

Aguilar has intriguing qualities in his ability to make off-platform throws and to layer the ball down the field with a quick release but as a much older prospect he will need to vastly improve his decision making and pocket presence to make an NFL roster.
Pavia has the intangibles desired at quarterback: a gamer mentality strong improv skills and good rushing ability but limited size arm strength and accuracy limit his upside at the next level.

With the experience ability to range across the defense and instincts playing the run Thomas will be able to provide sufficient depth but the lack of overall speed route recognition and inconsistent tackling strength will limit his overall usage.

Gumbs is a juiced-up EDGE rusher who plays with good leverage strong hands and a relentless motor but his inconsistency as a pass rusher and lack of awareness as a zone defender will make it difficult for him to be more than a depth-level backup at the next level.

Henry is a fast smaller wideout with impressive catching skills down the sideline but his struggles when it comes to working through contact and unpolished route running leave him with an uphill battle to getting sustained playing time.

Farmer is a guard-only prospect whose strength and power are the name of his game and while he has upside to eventually turn into a starter his heavy feet and ineffectiveness in space make him a backup-level player early on in his career.
Coogan is a smart steady interior presence who can win with body positioning and intelligence but lacks the power and overall play strength to be a consistent starter at the next level.
Nwaiwu is a powerful guard who uses leverage and proper positioning to win in the trenches but struggles with balance and lateral quickness limit his usage and effectiveness at the next level.
Harvey is an experienced corner that possesses lateral athleticism good patience and solid footwork that give him a decent foundation to potentially be a quality backup at the next level.
Wright has the size and anchor to plug up the middle of the line but his struggles with generating movement and second-level impact will keep him buried on the depth chart at the next level.
Kump is a large-framed experienced center prospect who will need to improve his tenacity and leverage to work himself into more than a backup center.
Rogers flashes receiving value as a safety blanket but his struggles winning separation battles as well as inconsistent blocking impact will leave him fighting for a roster spot.

Barnett is a strong powerful guard who has a nasty streak and the starting-level qualities to develop at the next level but his stiff lower half upright play style and lack of foot speed will likely prevent him from reaching his ceiling.
Roberts has the size length and functional strength every offensive line coach salivates over but must clean up his footwork awareness and body control to be more than a backup.

Cooper has the length power and strength but lets rushers into his chest far too often which showcases his mediocre hand usage and high pad level limiting him from having more success at the next level.

Cruz is a good linear athlete that pops out of his stance and gets to his landmarks in a hurry but his wide hands lack of power and inability to change directions will hold him back from being a reliable backup at either tackle spot early in his career.

Sharar is a fluid off-ball linebacker with improving instincts and a non-stop motor but he will need to overcome limited experience and improve his sturdiness in run support to avoid being a special teams only player.

Morris is a big powerful naturally strong guard with some starting-level qualities at the next level but a lack of foot quickness proper pad level and consistent hand placement ultimately make him a backup option early in his career.
Baynes has the ability to be a backup guard due to his durability power and awareness but his limited athletic profile and hand use issues will hold him back from being a starter.
Beerntsen is a compact but strong guard who has an abundance of experience under his belt who has the play strength and FBI to be a depth level guard at the next level.
Carmona is a durable and instinctual blocker who can fit into a zone-rushing scheme at the next level but lacks the reactive athleticism and body control to be a true versatile backup.

Ball is a big and bulky defensive tackle who will fit best as a true nose tackle with his ability to stuff the run but his athletic limitations and middling production as a pass rusher will keep him as a backup at the next level.
Banks is a naturally athletic rangy linebacker who shows potential but his processing speed will need to improve as will his ability to take on blocks in the interior.

Eason is a capable run defender with strong hands and solid pad level who can align from the nose or 3-tech spot but lacks the overall production and impact as a pass rusher to be an every-down contributor.
Trost’s frame leverage and positioning will allow him to get a chance at the next level but his age lack of tenacity and inconsistent hand usage could hold him back from hitting his ceiling.
Foster shows good FBI and upper-body strength but his limited lower-body quickness and lack of a leg drive will keep him as a backup in the NFL.
Braun has good size and length with solid footwork to work his way into proper positioning but he lacks play strength and isn’t nasty enough in the run game to be more than a backup-level lineman.
Dinkins is a tough aggressive blocker with soft hands who shows promise as a core special teamer but subpar balance and play strength will need to be improved as he works to the next level.
Wade has some intriguing athletic traits as an interior blocker but his inconsistency to set a strong early anchor will need to improve to consistently see more playing time.
DiGiorgio is a tall and physical interior blocker who plays with the desired mentality along the trenches but lacks the athleticism and control to be a versatile backup at the next level.

Sharp is an athletic tight end with good play speed who is still developing his all-around game as a tight end and if he can get stronger and polish up his blocking technique he can be a quality depth player.

Taylor is an experienced offensive lineman who has extensive experience to go along with power and awareness but inconsistent hand usage pad level and balance will make it difficult for him at the next level.

Hubbard has an explosive first step good instincts and a solid motor but his lack of strength bend and edge set ability will relegate him to be a low-end backup who needs more physical development to his frame.

Taylor is a tall lanky corner whose length and zone skills offer some growable upside at the next level but he’ll need to improve his hip fluidity and tackling in order to see the field on a consistent basis.

Sydnor is a long edge rusher who has the physical and athletic profile teams will covet but he is still a bit raw as a prospect and needs to add more core strength to allow him to be an every-down contributor.
Pyburn plays with the desired competitive toughness violence to defend the run and be a special teams contributor but his limitations rushing the passer will limit his role at the next level.

Robertson’s pass rush repertoire and flashes of effective bend around the corner make him an intriguing low-level depth option but his lack of explosiveness anchor and shed ability will limit his playing time at the next level.

Bredeson is an excellent run-blocker with the required toughness and technique to be an impactful fullback/H-back and core special teamer but his lack of pass-catching upside will limit his usage at the next level.

Kitselman has the frame and blocking ability to eventually become a complete tight end with further development but inconsistent hands and a lack of speed make him a depth-level option early in his career.

Jones is an athletic H-TE who brings some intriguing speed and explosiveness to the position but his lack of play strength and underwhelming size must improve to be a more well-rounded player to contribute in an offense.
Arkin is a stout TE that provides reliability as a blocker in both the pass and run game but has limitations as a pass catcher that will limit his role at the next level.
While he doesn’t fit the desired physical profile that most teams require O’Neill is a smart quick and high-effort edge rusher who can be a low-end backup at the next level.
Velling is a savvy receiving tight end with good size and hands but does not pose the mismatch threat or the blocking ability to fill the role of a true No. 2 H-TE.
Allen has solid size strength and zone awareness to make it as a back-end-of-the-roster player but limited speed tackling efficiency and versatility likely limit his potential at the next level.
Dawkins is an athletic center that shows good blocking range and a sufficient anchor but his lack of blocking power and effectiveness at the 2nd level will limit him to a backup-only role.
Gesky is a robust left guard that is both resilient and aware but his body control and balance concerns along with his lack of power in the run game will limit his ceiling at the next level.

Togiai offers a large frame movement fluidity and experience to NFL teams but his lack of refined hand usage and footwork combined with a limited positional profile suggests he will primarily serve in a backup role early in his career.
Harkey is a solidly-built lineman who plays with the desired power and play temperament but must improve his core strength and body control to become a quality backup at the next level.
Neal will use his wide frame strong upper body and experience to fight for a roster spot despite lacking high-level athletic traits quick feet and proper timing.

Dugger is a lengthy linebacker with reliable tackling and solid range but slow play recognition difficulty working through blocks and a lack of tenacity will likely leave him fighting for a roster spot.

Brockermeyer displays the intelligence and awareness that teams will look for in a center but his struggles against big physical defensive linemen will limit his overall effectiveness and keep him from starting at the next level.

Harsh is a versatile pass rushing EDGE who wins with his relentless motor and powerful hand use but will need to improve his run game discipline secondary moves and develop his ability to bend to get more playing time in the NFL.

James-Newby is able to win his rush with speed and finesse moves but is plagued with a lack of bend utilization of power moves and production in run support which will limit his usage at the next level.
Wright has NFL-level speed and leadership qualities but his thin frame shortcomings in play strength and tight lower body will limit him to being a low-end backup at the next level.
Pollard has the athleticism eye discipline and FBI to develop but his lack of coverage ability and strength to take on and shed blockers will keep him low on depth charts at the next level.

Nygra is a tough run-first center with adequate athleticism and anchor strength but his lack of length hand-fighting abilities and inconsistent leverage will limit him to a backup role at the next level.

Blay is a strong and tough run-stopping nose tackle that can anchor a defensive line but minimal production against the pass and struggles against double teams will make it tough for him to see consistent playing time at the next level.

Adams is a project who may take significant time to reach his ceiling given his issues as both a pass blocker and run blocker. That's thanks to mental lapses hand technique concerns and lack of footwork mirroring.
Catalon has consistently displayed resiliency fighting back through countless injuries but he possesses too many limitations to earn more than fringe roster consideration.

Heidenreich used his toughness functional athleticism and team-first approach to carve out a valuable role at the collegiate level but he will need to show he can be a reliable blocker in pass protection and create plays as a runner to earn NFL reps.

Connors is an intriguing prospect who is a natural hands-catcher and possesses toughness in spades but a lack of playmaking ability and reliability as a workhouse back may entice teams to work him into a fullback role.
Ott will look to show that his ceiling is what he showed during his first 2 seasons instead of the last 2 seasons which showed a non-factor in every area of the game.

Randall is a big-bodied WR-turned-RB who shows flashes of upside but he’s severely undeveloped as a RB as his feel as a runner and technique in pass pro need major work before he can be relied upon at the next level.
Montgomery is a size and speed-deficient wideout who makes up for it with his release package and savvy route running which will give him a shot to compete in a limited role.

Haynes is a short compact running back who has enough burst and receiving ability to be given a chance in a RB rotation but a lack of breakaway speed explosiveness and power limit his upside at the next level.
While undersized Reid will surely have a defined role at the next level as a gadgety playmaker but his inability to play with power and play through contact will limit his playing time.

Trayanum has the requisite vision burst and contact balance as well as special teams experience to earn his keep but a lack of passing-down utility and big-play upside will keep him toward the bottom of the depth chart at the next level.

Harden’s tough running style contact balance and pass game upside as both a blocker and receiver make him an intriguing depth-level back but limited speed elusiveness and vision will work against him in his fight to make a roster.

Allen is a punishing volume-heavy runner that produces with his vision and strength through contact but lack of top-end speed and impact in the pass game as a blocker or catcher will limit his usage at the NFL level.

Hilton is an underutilized receiver who possesses vertical speed and ability to track the ball well downfield but his lack of play strength and route running ability will make it an uphill battle for him to make a roster at the next level.
